---
title: "Final Project DATA 550"
author: "Seiryo Sasaki"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
```{r, setup, include = FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(gtsummary)
library(gt)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(here)
```

```{r, data_import, include = FALSE}
data <- read.csv(here("data", "country_data.csv"), header = TRUE)
```

# Introduction

This report analyzes a global data set encompassing `r nrow(data)` countries, 
covering key trends in economics, education, public health, and the environment.

The primary objective is to evaluate regional disparities across critical 
development indicators, specifically GDP, life expectancy, unemployment, homicide 
rates, CO2 emissions, and tourism. By identifying these gaps, the report aims to 
highlight how different geographic areas diverge in their developmental trajectories.
```{r, subset_data, include = FALSE}
subset_data <- readRDS(here("output", "subset_data.rds"))
```

# Table 1

Out of the original `r nrow(data)` countries, any with missing values were excluded.
This left a final subset of `r nrow(subset_data)` countries with complete data for 
all variables of interest.
```{r, table_1}
table_one <- readRDS(here("output", "table_one.rds"))
table_one
```

## Table 1 Description

Oceania reported the highest life expectancy for both males and females, as well
as the highest GDP per capita. While Africa recorded the highest unemployment rate
and total population, Asia emerged as the region with the highest population density.

# Graph 1
```{r, graph_1}
knitr::include_graphics(here("output", "graph_one.png"))
```

Africa represents the largest population in the data set, it contains 
`r nrow(subset_data %>% filter(region_group == "Africa"))` countries. Within the 
region, the three most populous countries are Nigeria, Ethiopia, and Egypt. The 
three least populous countries are Botswana, Tunisia, and Benin.
